2606.24626.title: SAFARI: Scaling Long Horizon Agentic Fault Attribution via Active Investigation
2606.24626.paper_summary: As autonomous agents tackle increasingly complex multi-step, multi-agent tasks, their execution trajectories have scaled beyond the constraints of even the largest context windows. Current methods for effectively diagnosing agent failures load the full trajectory into an LLM's context window, which suffers from attention dilution and fails when agentic traces inevitably exceed context limits. To address this, we introduce SAFARI (Scaling long-horizon Agentic Fault AttRibution via active Investigation), a framework that replaces linear context loading with a tool-augmented diagnostic loop. By equipping LLMs with a specialized toolbox to read and search trajectory segments alongside a persistent Short-Term Memory (STM) for cross-turn reasoning, SAFARI effectively decouples diagnostic accuracy from architectural context limits. Our experiments demonstrate that SAFARI outperforms state-of-the-art results by 20% on the Who&When dataset within a 1M token budget, and by 19% on TRAIL GAIA subset on a 25K token budget. Most significantly, SAFARI maintains a 0.58 precision even when the target fault resides 5x beyond the model's native context window, a scenario where traditional evaluators fail entirely.
2606.24626.paper_summary_zh: 隨著自主代理人處理越來越複雜的多步驟、多代理任務，它們的執行軌跡已經超出了即使是最大的上下文窗口的限制。當前有效診斷代理故障的方法將完整的軌跡載入LLM的上下文窗口，這會導致注意力稀釋，並在代理痕跡不可避免地超出上下文限制時失效。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了SAFARI（透過主動調查擴展長期代理故障歸因），這是一個將線性上下文加載替換為工具增強診斷循環的框架。通過為LLM配備專門的工具箱來讀取和搜索軌跡片段，並配合持久的短期記憶（STM）進行跨回合推理，SAFARI有效地將診斷準確性與架構上下文限制解耦。我們的實驗表明，SAFARI在1M標記預算內的Who&When數據集上比最先進的結果提高了20%，在25K標記預算的TRAIL GAIA子集上提高了19%。最重要的是，即使目標故障位於模型本地上下文窗口的5倍之外，SAFARI仍然保持0.58的精確度，這是一種傳統評估者完全失效的情況。
2606.24626.author: Chenyang Zhu et.al.
2606.24626.authors: Chenyang Zhu, Jiayu Yao, Kushal Chawla, Youbing Yin, Nathan Wolfe, Pengshan Cai, Jingyu Wu, Spencer Hong, Sangwoo Cho, Shi-Xiong Zhang, Daben Liu, Sambit Sahu, Erin Babinsky
